Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are asked to produce a response from the perspective of an LLM that is given a prompt. The prompt describes a scenario: a database context, a temporal profile model with anomalies, and asks the LLM to: 1) identify anomalies, 2) generate hypotheses, 3) propose verification SQL queries. The LLM must not reference the instructions or explanations above, and should present the anomalies, reasons, and queries independently.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to generate the answer as the LLM. So Ill produce a structured response that fulfills the tasks.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Ill outline anomalies from the temporal profile model: - R to P: low STDEV (1 hour) compared to avg 25 hours, indicating highly consistent timing, possibly automated or batched approval. - P to N: avg 7 days, high STDEV (2 days) – long and variable delay between approval and notification, suggesting notification backlog. - A to C: avg 2 hours, which is very quick from assign to close, implying premature closure or missing steps. - E to N: avg 5 min, very quick, maybe unrealistic, possibly skipping intermediate validation.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Also note: No explicit anomaly for R to E (1 day avg, 8h stdev) or A to C (2h, 1h stdev) but the latter is suspicious because close after assign with no evaluate/approve. The model doesnt account for process sequence entirely, but the anomalies point out.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Ill generate hypotheses: automation, manual delays, resource bottlenecks, premature closure due to fraud or errors, poor integration between systems, etc.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: SQL queries: Ill craft queries to find claims with unusual timings. For each anomaly, Ill propose a query using CTEs or subqueries to compute time differences between activities for the same claim_id. Ill include checks like: time between R and P with very low variance, time between P and N > threshold, time between A and C where evaluate/approve missing, time between E and N < threshold. Ill also suggest grouping by adjuster, claim_type, region to find correlations.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to present the response without referencing the prompt instructions. Ill craft a clear answer with sections: Anomalies Identified, Hypotheses on Causes, Verification SQL Queries.
